finding: People engage in self-serving dishonesty to profit while maintaining a positive self-concept through inattention to moral standards and categorization malleability.

Incidental tactile sensations from holding heavy/light, rough/smooth, or hard/soft objects nonconsciously influence judgments and decisions about unrelated people and situations in dimension-specific metaphor-consistent ways.
Body cues discriminate between intense positive and negative emotions while facial expressions do not; contextual body emotion shifts the perceived affect of nondiagnostic faces.

Recent encoding of novel information improves subsequent identification of subtle changes, while recent retrieval of familiar information increases integration of stored information into new memories, demonstrating lingering biases from prior episodic decisions.
Analytic processing—whether measured as individual differences or triggered by subtle manipulations—promotes religious disbelief.
In social-ecological systems with spatial-temporal resource dynamics, costly punishment alone does not increase gross benefits; it requires communication to be effective.
Engagement with literary fiction temporarily enhances theory of mind performance on tests of both affective and cognitive components compared to nonfiction, popular fiction, or control conditions.
Adults and 7-month-olds automatically encode and sustain others' beliefs with the same potency as their own beliefs, modulating behavior even after the agent departs.
Physical cleansing (hand washing) eliminates postdecisional dissonance effects by metaphorically providing a clean slate that removes past concerns.
Repeated imagined consumption of a food item reduces subsequent actual consumption through habituation, despite no direct sensory experience.
Test-restudy practice generates more effective mediators (retrievable, target-eliciting information) than restudy-only practice, explaining improved memory retention.
A brief expressive writing task about test worries immediately before a high-stakes exam improves exam performance, especially for chronically test-anxious students.
Scarcity reshapes attention allocation, causing deeper engagement with some problems while neglecting others, which explains behaviors like overborrowing that reinforce scarcity.
When people expect future access to information, they show lower recall of the information itself but enhanced recall for where to access it, establishing the Internet as a transactive memory system.
Being alone with one's thoughts without external stimuli is experienced as unpleasant; many people choose mild aversive stimulation over thought alone.

People unconsciously self-regulate social warmth deficits (loneliness) through physical warmth applications (warm baths/showers); physical and social warmth are substitutable despite lack of explicit awareness.
Goals activated outside conscious awareness (via priming) guide self-regulation and behavior effectively, demonstrating the same content-free features as consciously held goals.

Future events show anomalous retroactive influences on current responses (cognitive and affective), time-reversing established psychological effects; stimulus-seeking correlates with stronger psi performance.

Adopting high-power expansive postures causes increases in testosterone, decreases in cortisol, and increased feelings of power and risk tolerance; physiology and behavioral choices shift through embodied posturing.

Approach-motivated positive affect reduces attentional breadth, whereas low-approach-motivated positive affect broadens attention; approach motivation intensity modulates the attentional consequences of positive affect.
